why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:update_tags must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a body-consuming update_tags decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for one official empty tag-payload map fixture only and is separate from real tag contents, tag registry hydration, Configuration completion, Play entry, world load, render readiness, or runtime behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_update_tags_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 13, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x0d.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ClientboundUpdateTagsPacket(Map<ResourceKey<? extends Registry<?>>, TagNetworkSerialization.NetworkPayload>), ClientboundUpdateTagsPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundUpdateTagsPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundUpdateTagsPacket.getTags().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound update_tags dispatch/decode.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound mapping contains update_enabled_features id 0x0c, but not update_tags id 0x0d.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0xd in Clientbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration clientbound update_tags packet id 13
